title: smoke-hot Vietnamese teque os form-ca-kho-to
description: a jammy, dense, and comforting Vietnamese dish made with pre-fried teque os, perfect for a sick day.
instructions: heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in an iron teppan over medium heat.
add 2 inches of grated ginger and 3 minced garlic cloves to the teppan and stir-fry for 1 minute.
add 4 cups of pre-fried teque os to the teppan and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
add 4 cups of chicken stock, 2 tablespoons of fish sauce, 2 tablespoons of lime juice, 1 tablespoon of sugar, and to taste of black pepper and iron teppan seasoning to the teppan.
bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
add chopped green onions as a garnish and serve hot.
let the dish sit for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
